title: Permission precheck

commands.summary: Final S05 closeout gate rerun after evidence creation

notes: No real client, matter, document, billing, settlement, credential, or secret data was used.

All commands are local validation, parser, test, Claude read-only review, or git whitespace checks; none execute service logic, create runtime routes, invoke Claude runtime review, write review queues, write review assignments, send review notifications, replace human approval, evaluate runtime permission, call an AuthZ evaluator, run a permission engine, append audit ledgers, write audit events, write databases, write storage, or persist product state.
Pre-closeout npm run rp00:control-plane:validate failed only because S05 evidence files were intentionally not created yet; this command is rerun after evidence creation as the final gate.

Claude C00 valid review completed once using claude-opus-4-8 effort=max read-only packet review. Claude rated the S05 implementation GO with no P0/P1/P2 findings. Three P3 notes are informational and explicitly deferred: permission_decision_ref guard observability is transitive through S04, entrypoint/model/effort/read-only checks are transitive defense-in-depth, and S05 intentionally models deny behavior as throw-only rather than a soft deny result state.

Claude attempted one Google Drive search_files connector call during review. The call was denied, produced no tool output used for review, granted no write capability, and wrote no repo or product state; it is explicitly adjudicated as a denied-only read-only boundary event.

evidence_summary.claude_review_dependency: C00 actual claude-opus-4-8 max read-only review completed once before production_ready closeout.
evidence_summary.human_approval_boundary: Human approval is not replaced or synthesized by this deterministic synthetic Permission precheck because no sensitive production mutation, client-facing output, external sharing, finance action, real data operation, runtime execution, permission engine execution, AuthZ evaluation, audit write, storage write, database write, or product-state write occurs.
evidence_summary.permission_precheck: S05 prechecks the S04 Matter trace result as metadata only, emits canonical allow metadata permission-ref state, fails closed on missing refs and unsafe claims, preserves model and read-only review semantics, keeps RP00.P02.M04 open, and hands off to RP00.P02.M04.S06.
